lessons.title: Node.js Introduction
lessons.content:
What is Node.js?

  • Node.js is an open source server environment.
  • Node.js is free.
  • Node.js runs on various platforms (Windows, Linux, Unix, Mac OS X, etc.).
  • Node.js uses JavaScript on the server.

lessons.title: Node.js Get Started
lessons.content:
Download Node.js

Go to the official Node.js website and download the installer.

Node.js App

var http = require('http'); http.createServer(function (req, res) {   res.writeHead(200, {'Content-Type': 'text/plain'});   res.end('Hello World!'); }).listen(8080);

lessons.title: Node.js Modules
lessons.content:
What are Modules in Node.js?

Consider modules to be the same as JavaScript libraries.

A set of functions you want to include in your application.

To include a module, use the require() function with the name of the module:

var http = require('http');

quiz.question: Which of the following describes Node.js correctly?
options: A JavaScript runtime built on V8
